design_philosophy.core_loop: Charge rocket attack via directional input, execute multi-directional burst movement to navigate platforms and combat enemies, manage limited vitality and ammo
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (vitality/lives) combined with execution demands of precise rocket attack timing and directional control in combat scenarios
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Possum knight piloting a rocket pack as an elite warrior defending a kingdom; tactical repositioning through momentum-based movement
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rocket attack mechanic is the game's singular innovation—charging and releasing in eight directions creates a unique skill expression space distinct from standard platformers. Game commits fully to this system rather than offering alternative movement abilities. Designed by Nobuya Nakazato (Contra series), bringing that franchise's difficulty philosophy to a more mechanically focused platformer

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with 1UPs obtainable via item collection (spread throughout levels) or point thresholds (20,000 points for first 1UP, then every 60,000 points)
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues available on Normal/Hard difficulties; Very Hard (Crazy Hard in Japan) mode removes continues entirely, forcing single-run completion
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; player restarts at beginning of stage upon Game Over. No mid-stage checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four selectable difficulty tiers with escalating enemy damage and reduced starting lives/continues. Hardest difficulty (Very Hard) implements one-hit-death mechanic, eliminating vitality management and forcing perfect execution. Regional versions present difficulties differently (JP/EU: 2 options accessible via menu; NA: all 4 enabled by default)

progression.upgrade_system: No permanent upgrades documented. Vitality replenishment via consumables (apples, bananas) with scaling based on difficulty; banana items restore more vitality than apples
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven sequential stages, each composed of multiple sections ending in boss fight. Difficulty modes unlock conditionally by region
replayability_hooks: Four difficulty tiers with substantially different challenge profiles (especially Very Hard one-hit-death mode)
Point-based scoring system encouraging high-score runs
Alternate gameplay modes within linear structure (horizontal scrolling shooter sections, mech boss battle) create pacing variety
Difficulty-dependent vitality restoration and enemy damage create emergent skill expression opportunities

world_building_techniques: Prologue exposition establishing kingdom lore: King El Zephyrus defeated evil empire, sealed Pig Star starship, created Rocket Knight order to guard seal key
Mentor figure backstory: Sparkster trained by Mifune Sanjulo after being orphaned; previous conflict with Axel Gear establishes character relationships
Kidnapping plot device driving stage progression: Axel abducts Princess Sherry (keeper of seal key location), forcing pursuit across multiple kingdoms
Escalating threat structure: Invasion → pursuit → confrontation at Devotindos castle → space finale against Pig Star Core
Character reveals: False final boss (Devilgus revealed as robot), multiple antagonist encounters (Axel Gear as recurring rival)

boss_design.philosophy: Each of seven stages culminates in boss encounter; fifth stage features thematic variant (large robot mech duel with protagonist Axel Gear rather than traditional platformer boss). Boss encounters integrated into level narrative—Sparkster pursues Axel across geography, with Devotindos Empire forces serving as stage obstacles
notable_bosses: Axel Gear (recurring antagonist, multiple encounters including final melee duel)
Emperor Devilgus Devotindos (revealed as robot, primary antagonist)
Stage 5 boss (fought while controlling large robot mech, thematic departure from standard platforming)

faction_indicators: Rocket Knights: elite warrior order protecting Zephyrus kingdom, symbol of order/tradition
Devotindos Empire: invading pig-tribe faction seeking world-destroying Pig Star weapon
Axel Gear: corrupted Black Knight, former member turned antagonist, represents betrayal/ambition
danger_signals: Kidnapping of Princess Sherry (political leverage, escalation to violence)
Invasion of Zephyrus kingdom (military threat, loss of sovereignty)
Pig Star starship (WMD-equivalent, existential threat to multiple worlds)
Destruction of mentor figure Mifune (personal/organizational trauma for protagonist)
environmental_storytelling: Desert stage where Sparkster is blown by Axel (physical displacement, isolation)
Progression from kingdom castle → Devotindos territory → space fortress (geographical escalation of conflict)
Pig Star interior (technological/alien environment contrasting with kingdom setting)
